channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Brussels 20k: Over 560 people treated by Red Cross, 24 taken to hospital
item.description: In addition to water and refreshment stations, the Red Cross had set up ice baths for the first time to help overheated participants.

item.title: Open-air cinema and DJ sets: What's coming up in Brussels this summer?
item.description: From the world-renowned flower carpet to cinema under a starry sky, here are some of the unmissable events coming up in Brussels this summer.

item.title: Patrick Nimubona wins 20km Brussels run for second year in a row
item.description: Nimubona, who arrived in Brussels in 2014 as an asylum seeker, emerged victorious in a final sprint.

item.title: Brussels Airport to stop requiring removal of liquids and electronics in security checks
item.description: The security screening infrastructure at Brussels Airport in Zaventem will be completely revamped in the next few years, with new "cutting-edge" scanners.

item.title: A greener Sablon? What Brussels has in store for one of the city's most iconic squares
item.description: The City of Brussels has launched a redevelopment process for the Sablon area, promising a greener, more elegant and more accessible space.

item.title: A feast for the eyes: What is the Zinneke Parade and how did it come about?
item.description: The biennial parade has been described as "an integral part of Brussels' heritage".

item.title: Brussels 20 km to feature 'ice baths' amid heatstroke fears
item.description: Organisers of Sunday's race have stepped up preparations to ensure that runners remain safe from the heat.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 29 - 31 May
item.description: From art and music festivals and cultural heritage to English-language comedy and bargain hunting, there's something for everyone this weekend.

item.title: Woman critically injured in stabbing in Molenbeek
item.description: Bystanders managed to restrain the suspected perpetrator, who was then detained by the police.

item.title: Large fire breaks out at waste treatment site in Brussels
item.description: The fire started on a pile of scrap metal.

item.title: 'Unfeasible': Interpreters denounce pay rates for asylum-related assignments in Brussels
item.description: The offers require full-time, on-site presence, fixed working hours, and long-term commitments for a gross hourly rate of approximately €16.

item.title: Hot weather and evening thunderstorms to hit Belgium
item.description: The Royal Meteorological Institute (RMI) has issued a yellow heat warning across Belgium, including Brussels.

item.title: Brussels one step closer to new public open-air rooftop swimming pool
item.description: The open-air swimming pool is expected to have a surface area similar to that of Brussels' Grand Place.

item.title: Brussels for kids: What to do with your family this weekend – 30 to 31 May
item.description: From parades and festivals to circus classes and urban farms visits, there are lots of fun activities for kids to enjoy in Brussels this weekend.

item.title: Belgian historian chosen to herald during Brussels Ommegang
item.description: Bart Van Loo will narrate the history of Brussels, Emperor Charles V, and the Burgundian Netherlands for spectators during Ommegang 2026 celebrations.

item.title: Princess Elisabeth on life at Harvard and what she misses most about Belgium
item.description: The Belgian princess has given her first real interview to the written press, speaking to Le Soir and three other Belgian newspapers just before her graduation from Harvard University.

item.title: Mother arrested for attempting to kill her three children in Uccle
item.description: Neighbours called emergency services around 20:30 after hearing screams at the family's home on Avenue Brugmann in Brussels.

item.title: Brussels Government approves 'coercive control' of Saint-Josse municipality
item.description: In addition to the financial problems, serious shortcomings were also found at the Saint-Josse social housing agency.

item.title: Two key train lines around Brussels will not run this weekend
item.description: This is due to maintenance works by the railway infrastructure operator Infrabel.

item.title: Brussels police conduct searches at social housing operator in Anderlecht
item.description: The Foyer Anderlechtois has been searched in light of the Flemish public media's investigation into the allocation of social housing in Anderlecht.
